title: penstemon occiduus
intro: Penstemon occiduus, also known as the Desert Penstemon or California Purple Sage, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States, particularly in California and Arizona. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Penstemon occiduus have been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems, offering health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, with the leaves and stems being used to make herbal preparations.
